sensors.name: HC-SR04 Ultrasonic Sensor
sensors.description: Ultrasonic distance sensor. Range 2-400cm. Accuracy ~3mm. Uses sound waves. Needs 5V supply but 3.3V signal is TTL compatible.
common_issues: Echo pin needs voltage divider (5V to 3.3V)
Minimum detection distance ~2cm
Soft surfaces absorb sound waves
Temperature affects accuracy

sensors.name: DHT22 (AM2302) Temperature/Humidity
sensors.pins: Data=GPIO, VCC=3.3V/5V, GND (add 10K pull-up resistor on data line)
sensors.library: DHT sensor library (Arduino) / esp-idf dht driver
sensors.description: Temperature (0-80C, +-0.5C) and humidity (0-100%, +-2%) sensor. Read every 2 seconds minimum. Protocol is timing-sensitive.
common_issues: Must wait 2 seconds between reads
Pull-up resistor required on data line
Long cables cause unreliable readings
Heats up slightly from own power consumption

sensors.name: BMP280 / BMP180 Barometric Pressure
sensors.pins: I2C: SDA, SCL, VCC=3.3V, GND. SPI: MOSI, MISO, SCK, CS
sensors.description: Barometric pressure and temperature sensor. BMP280: I2C@0x77 or 0x76, SPI 10MHz max. Altitude accuracy ~1m. BMP180 is older, slower, less accurate.
common_issues: Wrong I2C address (0x76 vs 0x77)
Needs 3.3V (not 5V) - no onboard regulator on some modules
Compensation needed for altitude calculation
I2C speed should be 400kHz or lower

sensors.name: MPU-6050 Accelerometer/Gyroscope
sensors.pins: SDA, SCL, VCC=3.3V, GND, INT for interrupt
sensors.description: 6-axis IMU with 3-axis gyro and 3-axis accelerometer. I2C@0x68 or 0x69. Gyro range up to 2000 deg/s. Accel range up to 16g.
common_issues: I2C address conflict with other devices at 0x68
Sensor bias/drift needs calibration
Mounting orientation affects readings
High I2C speed can cause read errors

sensors.name: DS18B20 One-Wire Temperature
sensors.description: Waterproof temperature probe. Range -55 to 125C, accuracy +-0.5C. Supports multiple sensors on single wire (parasitic power mode available). Each sensor has unique 64-bit ID.
common_issues: Strong pull-up needed for parasitic power
Long wires cause communication errors
Multiple sensors need ROM search to identify
Conversion time ~750ms at 12-bit resolution

sensors.name: ADS1115 16-bit ADC
sensors.pins: SDA, SCL, VCC=3.3V-5V, GND. A0-A3 for analog inputs
sensors.description: 16-bit 4-channel ADC with programmable gain. I2C@0x48-0x4B. Supports single-ended and differential measurements. 860 samples/second. Great for reading sensors that need more precision than ESP32's ADC.
common_issues: Default address may conflict with other sensors
PGA gain must match expected voltage range
I2C address selection via ADDR pin
Single-ended channels share ground reference

sensors.name: BH1750 Ambient Light Sensor
sensors.description: Digital ambient light sensor. I2C@0x23 or 0x5C. Measures lux (0-65535). Spectral response close to human eye. No infrared sensitivity. Great for automatic brightness control.
common_issues: Auto-ranging takes multiple readings
Needs calibration for accurate lux values
Window/aperture must be clean
I2C address selection on board

sensors.name: SSD1306 OLED Display 128x64
sensors.pins: I2C: SDA, SCL, VCC=3.3V, GND (address 0x3C or 0x3D). SPI: MOSI, SCK, DC, RESET, CS
sensors.description: 128x64 monochrome OLED display. I2C is simpler (2 wires). SPI is faster. Memory: 1KB (128x64/8). u8g2 library supports both. Fonts and bitmaps consume RAM quickly.
common_issues: Display not clearing properly between updates
I2C address 0x3C vs 0x3D depending on board
RAM usage high with large fonts
SPI requires more GPIO pins but is faster

sensors.name: INA219 Current/Voltage Sensor
sensors.pins: SDA, SCL, VCC=3.3V-5V, GND. V+ and V- for load
sensors.description: High-side current sensor with 12-bit resolution. I2C@0x40-0x4F. Measures voltage (0-26V), current, and power. Requires shunt resistor (typically 0.1 ohm). Accuracy depends on shunt value.
common_issues: Wrong shunt resistor value in library config
Maximum current limited by shunt power rating
V- must be connected to load ground
Bus voltage must be within 3-26V range

sensors.name: RC522 RFID Reader
sensors.pins: MOSI, MISO, SCK, SDA (CS), RST. VCC=3.3V (NOT 5V!)
sensors.description: 13.56MHz RFID reader. Reads MIFARE Classic cards/tags. SPI at 10MHz. Power with 3.3V only - 5V damages it. Range ~3-5cm. Each tag has unique UID (4-7 bytes).
common_issues: Powering with 5V destroys the module
SPI connections need correct pin mapping
Tags need to be close (2-3cm) for reliable reads
Not all MIFARE tag types supported

sensors.name: NRF24L01 Wireless Transceiver
sensors.pins: MOSI, MISO, SCK, CSN, CE, IRQ (optional). VCC=3.3V (NOT 5V!)
sensors.description: 2.4GHz wireless transceiver. Range up to 100m (with PA+LNA module). SPI up to 10MHz. Requires capacitors across VCC-GND for stability. Pipes for multi-point communication.
common_issues: Power supply insufficient - use 3.3V and add 10uF cap
PA+LNA modules draw more current - need external supply
CE and CSN pins must be different
SPI speed too high causes communication errors

sensors.name: VL53L0X Time-of-Flight Distance
sensors.pins: SDA, SCL, VCC=2.8V-5V (onboard regulator), GND, XSHUT (shutdown)
sensors.description: Laser-based time-of-flight distance sensor. I2C@0x29. Range 50-1200mm. Accuracy +-3%. Much better than ultrasonic. Immune to surface color/texture (vs ultrasonic).
common_issues: I2C address conflict when using multiple sensors
Needs 2.8V (has onboard regulator so 3.3V or 5V works)
Needs calibration for accurate absolute distance
Aperture must not be covered
